speaker: Dhritarashtra
essence: The war begins in a question, not a sword strike.
snippet: Dhritarashtra said: O Sanjaya, what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do, assembled on the holy field of Kurukshetra and eager for battle?

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: War becomes real the moment the enemy is seen clearly.
snippet: Sanjaya said: Seeing the Pandava army arranged in battle formation, King Duryodhana went to Dronacharya and spoke these words.

speaker: Duryodhana
essence: A battlefield can be won first by how it is described.
snippet: O teacher, look at this great army of the sons of Pandu, arranged in battle formation by your wise disciple, the son of Drupada.

speaker: Duryodhana
essence: The other side is strong, and pretending otherwise changes nothing.
snippet: Here in the Pandava army are mighty warriors, great archers, equal to Bhima and Arjuna in battle: Satyaki, Virata, and the great chariot-…

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: The battlefield is already full of power and named allies.
snippet: Dhृष्टकेतु, Chekitana, and the valiant king of Kashi; Purujit, Kuntibhoja, and Shaibya, the best among men.

speaker: Duryodhana
essence: The enemy line is full of elite fighters.
snippet: Yudhamanyu, brave and powerful, Uttamaujas, brave and powerful, Subhadra's son, and the sons of Draupadi — all of them are great warriors.

speaker: Duryodhana
essence: Pride counts its own strength when fear is near.
snippet: O best of the twice-born, note also our own chief warriors. I name them to remind you of our army.

speaker: Duryodhana
essence: Fear clings to strength by naming it aloud.
snippet: Bhishma, Karna, Kripa, Ashvatthama, Vikarna, and the son of Somadatta are also there.

speaker: Duryodhana
essence: Confidence built on numbers still trembles before battle.
snippet: Besides these, many heroes have given up their lives for my sake. They wield many kinds of weapons and are all skilled in warfare.

speaker: Duryodhana
essence: Fear can declare defeat before the battle starts.
snippet: Our force, guarded by Bhishma, is insufficient; their force, guarded by Bhima, is sufficient.

speaker: Duryodhana
essence: Victory begins with disciplined formation, not scattered strength.
snippet: Remain at your stations on every front and protect Bhishma from all sides.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: A single battle cry can turn fear into momentum.
snippet: The elder of the Kuru clan, the powerful grandfather Bhishma, roared like a lion and blew his conch loudly, filling Duryodhana with joy.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: The war announces itself before the first blow.
snippet: Then conches, kettledrums, drums, and trumpets burst forth at once. Their sound became thunderous.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: Resolve answers chaos before words begin.
snippet: Then, seated on the great chariot yoked with white horses, Madhava and Pandava blew their divine conches.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: The war begins in sound before a single strike.
snippet: Hrisikesha Krishna blew the Panchajanya, Dhananjaya Arjuna blew the Devadatta, and Bhima of mighty deeds blew the great conch Paundra.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: The war begins to answer back in sound.
snippet: King Yudhishthira, son of Kunti, blew the Anantavijaya; Nakula and Sahadeva blew the Sughosha and Manipushpaka.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: Names and conches turn hesitation into commitment.
snippet: The great archer from Kashi, Shikhandi the great warrior, Dhrishtadyumna, Virata, and invincible Satyaki.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: Allied resolve announces itself before the battle starts.
snippet: Drupada, Draupadi's sons, and mighty Abhimanyu each blew their conches separately, O lord of the earth.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: A single sound can break resolve before battle begins.
snippet: That terrible sound split the hearts of Dhritarashtra's side, echoing through sky and earth.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: The battle reaches its threshold, and action begins before words do.
snippet: Then, seeing the sons of Dhritarashtra standing in battle order while the weapons were about to begin, Arjuna lifted his bow.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Clear sight comes before decisive action.
snippet: Arjuna said: O Hrishikesha, place my chariot between the two armies, O Achyuta, until I can look at these men who wish to fight and see w…

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Clear seeing comes before irreversible action.
snippet: Arjuna said: O Achyuta, keep my chariot between the two armies until I can inspect these men standing here, eager for battle, and see wit…

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Conflict becomes real when you look at the people involved.
snippet: I want to see those who have gathered here, eager to fight and wishing to please the evil-minded son of Dhritarashtra.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: The chariot is placed where choice becomes unavoidable.
snippet: Sanjaya said: Spoken to thus by Gudakesha, Hrishikesha stationed the finest chariot between the two armies, in front of Bhishma, Drona, a…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Seeing the people involved makes denial impossible.
snippet: Standing before Bhishma, Drona, and all the rulers, Krishna said, "O Partha, behold these assembled Kurus."

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: Recognition turns battle into sorrow.
snippet: Then Arjuna, son of Pritha, saw fathers, grandfathers, teachers, maternal uncles, brothers, sons, grandsons, friends, fathers-in-law, and…

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: The battle becomes unbearable when every side is family.
snippet: Seeing all his relatives standing there, Arjuna sank into grief and spoke in fear.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Compassion can stop the warrior before the first arrow flies.
snippet: Arjuna said: Seeing my own people gathered here, eager for battle, I am overcome with pity and grief, O Krishna.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: The body reveals fear before the mind can explain it.
snippet: My limbs are failing, my mouth is drying up, my body trembles, and my hair stands on end.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Fear strips away control before a single arrow is released.
snippet: My bow slips from my hand. My skin burns. I cannot stand steady, and my mind reels.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: A victory that destroys your own people is not worth having.
snippet: O Keshava, I see only inauspicious signs, and I see no good in killing my own people in battle.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Victory means nothing when the price is the destruction of what you love.
snippet: I do not desire victory, Krishna, nor kingdom, nor pleasures. What use is kingdom to us, Govinda? What use are pleasures or even life?

speaker: Arjuna
essence: The prize is empty when the people attached to it stand in the line of fire.
snippet: Those for whom we wanted kingdom, pleasures, and comfort stand here in battle, ready to give up life and wealth.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Blood ties make victory feel like loss.
snippet: Teachers, fathers, sons, grandfathers, maternal uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law, and other relatives.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: No prize can justify killing what is sacred to you.
snippet: I do not want to kill these people, even if they kill me, O Madhusudana. Not even for the rule of the three worlds, much less for the earth.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Violence against kin brings no joy; it only deepens the wound.
snippet: O Janardana, what joy would come to us by killing these sons of Dhritarashtra? Killing these aggressors would only bring us sin.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Kinship makes violence feel unbearable.
snippet: Therefore, we are not fit to kill our own kinsmen, the sons of Dhritarashtra. How could we be happy after killing our relatives, O Madhava?

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Greed blinds people to the damage they are causing.
snippet: Though greed has blinded them, they do not see the fault in destroying a family or the wrong in betraying friends.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Clear sight makes withdrawal from harm the only sane choice.
snippet: Why should we not turn away from this sin, when we clearly see the fault in destroying the family, O Janardana?

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Destroy the family, and the moral order inside it collapses.
snippet: When a family falls apart, its ancient duties disappear. When duty is lost, wrongdoing overtakes the whole family.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: When order breaks, the damage spreads into future generations.
snippet: When lawlessness grows, the women of the family are corrupted. When women are corrupted, mixed social disorder is born.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Breaking family order is imagined as ruin that reaches even the dead.
snippet: Mixed lineage leads the destroyers of family to ruin, and their ancestors fall because offerings of food and water stop.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: When conduct breaks, the inherited order that protects everyone breaks too.
snippet: Because of these faults that create mixed social order, the eternal duties of family and community are destroyed.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Breaking family duty brings suffering that lasts beyond one lifetime.
snippet: O Janardana, we have always heard that those whose family duties are destroyed dwell long in a lower realm.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Greed can make even victory feel like a crime.
snippet: Alas, we have resolved to commit a grave wrong—driven by greed for kingdom and pleasure, we are ready to kill our own kin.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Harming loved ones feels worse than losing your own life.
snippet: If the armed sons of Dhritarashtra were to kill me in battle while I stood unresisting and without weapons, that would be far better for me.

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: Grief can make even a warrior lay down his weapons.
snippet: Sanjaya said: Saying this, Arjuna sat down on the chariot seat in the midst of the battlefield, casting aside his bow and arrows, his min…

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: Grief opens the door for instruction.
snippet: Sanjaya said: To Arjuna, overcome by pity, his eyes full of tears and clouded with grief, Madhusudana spoke these words.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Delusion has no place in a decisive moment.
snippet: Krishna said: Arjuna, where has this delusion come from in this crisis? Noble people do not follow it. It does not lead upward, and it br…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Weakness is optional; the call to act remains.
snippet: Do not yield to weakness, Partha. It does not suit you. Cast off this petty weakness of heart and rise, scorcher of foes.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Respect can make necessary action feel unbearable.
snippet: Arjuna said: How can I fight Bhishma and Drona with arrows in battle, O Madhusudana? They are worthy of worship, O destroyer of enemies.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: A victory that destroys reverence is already a loss.
snippet: It is better to live on alms in this world than to kill these great teachers. If I kill them, the pleasures I enjoy here will be stained …

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Honest uncertainty is the first crack in moral paralysis.
snippet: We do not know which is better for us: to fight or not to fight. We do not even know whether we will conquer them or they us. The sons of…

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Clarity begins when you admit you cannot see the right action alone.
snippet: My nature is overwhelmed by weakness, and my mind is confused about duty. I ask you: tell me with certainty what is best for me. I am you…

speaker: Arjuna
essence: No outer victory can cure an inner collapse.
snippet: Even if I gained an unrivaled, prosperous kingdom on earth or lordship over the gods, I see nothing that could remove this grief that dri…

speaker: Sanjaya
essence: Refusal has stopped the war inside him.
snippet: Sanjaya said: Having spoken thus to Hrishikesha, Gudakesha, the scorcher of foes, said to Govinda, “I will not fight,” and fell silent.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Guidance begins when grief is met without rejection.
snippet: Hṛṣīkeśa spoke to the grieving Arjuna, smiling slightly, in the midst of the two armies.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear seeing ends grief where the wise know none is needed.
snippet: The Blessed Lord said: You grieve for what should not be grieved for, yet speak words of wisdom. The wise do not grieve for the dead or t…

speaker: Krishna
essence: What truly exists was never born and never lost.
snippet: I was never absent, nor were you, nor these kings. Nor will any of us cease to be in the future.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What changes form is not what you truly are.
snippet: Just as the embodied one passes through childhood, youth, and old age in this body, so too it passes into another body. The wise are not …

speaker: Krishna
essence: Passing sensations lose power when you stop treating them as permanent.
snippet: Contacts with the senses, O son of Kunti, bring heat and cold, pleasure and pain. They come and go; they are impermanent. Endure them, O …

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom begins where pain and pleasure stop controlling you.
snippet: The wise person who is not shaken by these, and remains equal in pain and pleasure, becomes fit for freedom.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What passes away was never ultimate; what is real cannot vanish.
snippet: The unreal has no being; the real has no nonbeing. Seers have understood the boundary of both.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What truly pervades everything cannot be destroyed.
snippet: Know that to be imperishable by which this entire world is pervaded. No one can destroy this imperishable reality.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What is truly you cannot be destroyed, so duty remains.
snippet: These bodies are said to be finite, but the embodied being is eternal, indestructible, and immeasurable. Therefore, fight, Arjuna.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What you are cannot be reduced to killer or victim.
snippet: Whoever thinks this imperishable being is the killer, and whoever thinks it is killed, both do not understand. It neither kills nor is ki…

speaker: Krishna
essence: What you are cannot be touched by birth or death.
snippet: It is never born and never dies. It did not come into being, and it will not cease. Unborn, eternal, everlasting, ancient, it is not dest…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear seeing makes violence look absurd.
snippet: One who knows the imperishable, eternal, unborn, and unchanging cannot think of killing or causing death.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What you are does not end when one body is left behind.
snippet: As a person discards worn-out clothes and puts on new ones, the embodied being discards worn-out bodies and enters new ones.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Your deepest reality is beyond every physical force.
snippet: Weapons cannot cut it, fire cannot burn it, water cannot wet it, and wind cannot dry it.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What you are cannot be harmed by anything that changes.
snippet: This embodied self cannot be cut, burned, wetted, or dried. It is eternal, all-pervading, unmoving, fixed, and timeless.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What is deepest in you was never available for loss.
snippet: It is unseen, beyond thought, and said to be unchanged. Therefore, knowing it this way, you should not grieve.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Even the plain fact of change is no reason to drown in sorrow.
snippet: O mighty-armed one, even if you think this embodied being is always born and always dies, you still should not grieve like this.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What must change is not a reason for grief.
snippet: For what is born, death is certain; for what dies, birth is certain. What cannot be avoided should not be grieved over.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What appears briefly was never meant to be clung to.
snippet: All beings are unmanifest before birth and unmanifest after death; they are manifest only in between. Why grieve over that?

speaker: Krishna
essence: The true self remains mysterious even after endless hearing.
snippet: Some behold the inner being as wondrous; some speak of it as wondrous; some hear of it as wondrous; and even after hearing, no one truly …

speaker: Krishna
essence: What is most real in a person cannot be killed, so grief loses its ground.
snippet: O descendant of Bharata, the embodied one in every body is eternal and cannot be slain. Therefore you should not grieve for any being.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Right action can feel hard and still be the best choice.
snippet: Considering your own duty, you should not waver. For a warrior, no action is more beneficial than a righteous battle.

speaker: Krishna
essence: A righteous burden can become the highest opening.
snippet: An unbidden battle is an open gate to higher realms. O Partha, fortunate are the warriors who receive such a war.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Avoiding the right duty does not protect you; it stains you.
snippet: If you do not fight this righteous war, you will abandon your duty and honor and incur blame.

speaker: Krishna
essence: A honored life can be wounded more by shame than by death.
snippet: And all beings will speak of your everlasting disgrace. For an honored person, disgrace is worse than death.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Retreat from duty, and respect turns to shrinkage.
snippet: The great warriors will think you withdrew from battle out of fear. Those who once held you in high regard will see you as small.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Public shame can hurt more than physical danger.
snippet: Your enemies will speak many unworthy words, mocking your strength. What could be more painful than that?

speaker: Krishna
essence: Act now; the result does not cancel the duty.
snippet: If you are killed, you will attain heaven; if you win, you will enjoy the kingdom. Therefore, rise, Kaunteya, and fight with resolve.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Equanimity frees action from the stain of panic and craving.
snippet: Treat pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat alike. Then enter the battle; you will not incur wrongdoing.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear understanding frees action from bondage.
snippet: This wisdom has been taught to you in Sankhya. Now hear it in yoga. Equipped with this understanding, Arjuna, you will cast off the bonda…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Even a tiny start on the right path protects you from fear.
snippet: In this human world, no effort toward this steady wisdom is ever lost. No harmful result comes from it. Even a little practice of this dh…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Resolute seeing unifies the mind; hesitation multiplies it.
snippet: O son of the Kurus, the resolute intellect is one-pointed here. The intellect of the irresolute is many-branched and endless.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Beautiful words can hide a mind trapped by desire.
snippet: The unwise speak flowery words. They delight in the ritual sections of the Vedas and say nothing else exists.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Flowery promises can keep desire alive while pretending to guide you.
snippet: Those without clear seeing speak flowery words that promise birth and action-fruit, and describe many rites for enjoyment and power.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Craving scatters the mind before wisdom can settle.
snippet: For those whose minds are carried away by pleasure and power, steadfast determination in meditation does not arise.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom begins when gain and loss stop defining you.
snippet: The Vedas deal with the three qualities. Arjuna, rise beyond the three qualities, beyond opposites, fixed in lasting reality, free from c…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Complete understanding makes lesser sources unnecessary.
snippet: For one who understands, all the Vedas are as useful as a small puddle when a great reservoir of water is at hand.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Action belongs to you; the result does not.
snippet: You have a right to action alone, never to its fruits. Do not become the cause of the result, and do not cling to inaction.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Equanimity turns action into yoga.
snippet: O Dhananjaya, perform actions while established in yoga, abandoning attachment. Remain equal in success and failure, for equanimity is ca…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear discernment is richer than reward-chasing.
snippet: Action driven by craving is far below the steadiness of clear discernment. Seek refuge in clear discernment, for those who chase results …

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear action frees you from the weight of both success and failure.
snippet: One who is united with clear discernment casts off both good and bad deeds in this life. Therefore, devote yourself to yoga, for yoga is …

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom comes when action is done without clutching its reward.
snippet: Wise people, united in clarity, abandon the fruit born of action and reach the flawless state, free from the bondage of birth.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear seeing ends the pull of future pleasures.
snippet: When your intellect crosses the swamp of delusion, you will grow dispassionate toward what has been heard and what remains to be heard.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Yoga begins when the mind stops being pulled in every direction.
snippet: When your intellect, unmoved by conflicting teachings, becomes steady in complete stillness, you will attain yoga.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Steady wisdom must be recognizable in ordinary movement.
snippet: Arjuna said: O Kesava, what are the marks of one whose wisdom is steady and who is absorbed in stillness? How does a person of steady und…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Steadiness begins when desire no longer defines your sense of enough.
snippet: The divine said: When a person gives up all desires that arise in the mind, and is satisfied in the true self by the true self alone, tha…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Steadiness remains when pleasure and pain lose their grip.
snippet: The steady-minded sage is unshaken by sorrow, free from craving in joy, and beyond attachment, fear, and anger.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Steady wisdom does not need life to feel favorable.
snippet: One who feels no clinging anywhere, and neither rejoices nor hates when good or bad comes, has steady wisdom.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Steadiness begins when the senses stop running outward.
snippet: When this one draws the senses fully back from sense-objects, like a tortoise withdrawing its limbs, wisdom is steady.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Craving ends only when a higher reality becomes more compelling.
snippet: Objects fall away from the self-denying person, but the taste for them remains. For the one who has seen the supreme reality, even that t…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Restraint fails without disciplined senses.
snippet: Even for a wise person who strives, the turbulent senses can forcibly carry away the mind.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Wisdom becomes steady only when the senses are no longer in charge.
snippet: Having restrained all the senses, the disciplined one should sit devoted to Me. For one whose senses are under control, wisdom is firmly …

speaker: Krishna
essence: What you dwell on becomes what you cling to, then what burns you.
snippet: Thinking about sense objects creates attachment to them. Attachment gives rise to desire; desire gives rise to anger.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Anger first distorts seeing, then destroys the mind that should guide action.
snippet: From anger comes delusion; from delusion, confusion of memory; from confusion of memory, loss of discernment; from loss of discernment, r…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom comes from meeting experience without attraction or aversion.
snippet: The disciplined person, moving among objects with senses under control and free from attraction and aversion, attains serenity.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear seeing ends suffering and lets understanding settle at once.
snippet: In inner clarity, all suffering falls away. For one whose mind is clear, understanding settles quickly.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Without inner discipline, even happiness has no place to stand.
snippet: A restless mind has no steady discernment, no steady purpose, no peace, and without peace, no happiness.

speaker: Krishna
essence: One uncontrolled sense can carry the mind away.
snippet: As the winds carry a boat on water, even one roaming sense can sweep away the mind's discernment.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Steady wisdom begins when the senses stop ruling your attention.
snippet: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, the wisdom of one whose senses are fully restrained from sense-objects is steady.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What the many pursue in sleep, the wise leave behind awake.
snippet: What is night for all beings is wakefulness for the self-controlled one. What all beings wake toward is night for the seer who knows the …

speaker: Krishna
essence: Peace belongs to the one who stays steady while desire moves.
snippet: As rivers flow into the sea and the sea remains steady, so desires may enter a disciplined person without disturbing inner peace. The per…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Peace begins when wanting, owning, and self-importance fall away.
snippet: One who gives up all desires, moves without craving, without possessiveness, and without ego, attains peace.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Steadiness in the supreme reality ends confusion, even at life’s edge.
snippet: Arjuna, this is the Brahmic state. Having reached it, one is never deluded. Remaining established in it even at the end of life, one atta…

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Mixed guidance deepens paralysis; clarity must come before action.
snippet: Arjuna said: O Janardana, if you consider understanding superior to action, then why do you urge me to this terrible action, O Keshava? Y…

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Confusion ends when the mind asks for one clear direction.
snippet: Arjuna said: Your words seem mixed and confusing to me. Tell me one thing clearly, so I may find the highest good.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Different natures are steadied by different disciplines.
snippet: The divine said: In this world, I have already taught two kinds of steadfast practice: the path of knowledge for the contemplative, and t…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom is not found by escaping action, but by changing your relation to it.
snippet: A person does not reach actionless freedom by avoiding action, nor by renouncing action alone does one reach perfection.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Inaction is an illusion; nature is already moving you.
snippet: No one can remain without action even for a moment. Everyone is driven to act by the qualities born of nature.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Outer restraint means nothing if the mind still feeds desire.
snippet: One who restrains the organs of action but keeps thinking of sense objects in the mind is deluded and called a hypocrite.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Self-mastery makes action noble, not the outer task alone.
snippet: Arjuna, the one who restrains the senses with the mind and begins karma yoga without attachment is श्रेष्ठ.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Right action is unavoidable; even survival depends on it.
snippet: Do the duty prescribed for you. Action is better than inaction, and even your body cannot be sustained by inaction.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Work becomes bondage when it is done for yourself.
snippet: Actions done for sacrifice free a person; actions done for personal gain bind. Therefore, Arjuna, do your duty without attachment for tha…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Life flourishes when duty becomes mutual support.
snippet: In the beginning, Prajapati created beings together with sacrifice and said: “By this, you shall prosper. May this sacrifice be the wish-…

speaker: Unknown
essence: Your duty nourishes the whole, and the whole nourishes you back.
snippet: By nurturing the radiant beings through this duty, let them nurture you. Nurturing one another, you will attain the highest good.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What is received must be offered back, or it becomes theft.
snippet: The radiant beings, nourished by sacrifice, will give you the enjoyments you need. One who enjoys their gifts without offering back is a …

speaker: Krishna
essence: What is offered purifies; what is hoarded for the self corrupts.
snippet: Those who eat the leftovers of sacrifice are freed from all faults. Those who cook only for themselves eat only wrongdoing.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Action sustains the cycle that makes life possible.
snippet: All beings arise from food. Food comes from rain. Rain comes from sacrifice. Sacrifice arises from action.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Right action is not random; it rests in a deeper, sustaining order.
snippet: Know that action comes from the Vedas, and the Vedas come from the imperishable. Therefore, the all-pervading supreme reality is always e…

speaker: Krishna
essence: A life detached from duty empties itself from within.
snippet: O Partha, the one who does not follow this cycle set in motion here lives in vain, delighting only in the senses and living a life of wro…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Fulfillment in the true self ends the need for striving.
snippet: The person who delights in the true self, is satisfied in the true self, and is content in the true self has no duty left.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom ends the need to profit from action.
snippet: For the perfected person, no purpose remains in doing, and no purpose remains in not doing. No being in the world is a source of personal…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom comes when action is done without grasping at its reward.
snippet: Therefore, always perform your duty without attachment; by acting without attachment, a person reaches the supreme.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Perfection comes through action that serves more than the self.
snippet: Janaka and others attained perfection through action itself. You should act too, while keeping the welfare of the world in view.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The highest standard is not spoken; it is copied.
snippet: Whatever the best person does, others follow. Whatever standard they set, the world copies.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Even the fulfilled keep working, because action need not come from lack.
snippet: O Partha, there is nothing in the three worlds that I must do, and nothing I have not yet gained that must be gained. Yet I remain engage…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Careless withdrawal by the highest would unravel everyone below.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: Wisdom keeps acting, but never for personal gain.
snippet: As the ignorant act with attachment to action, so should the wise act without attachment, seeking the welfare of the world.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: The doer is imagined; action belongs to nature's forces.
snippet: All actions are carried out in every way by the qualities of nature. Deluded by ego, a person thinks, 'I am the doer.'

speaker: Krishna
essence: Seeing action as nature's movement ends attachment.
snippet: The one who truly understands the division of qualities and actions does not cling, seeing that qualities act upon qualities.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clarity should guide confusion, not crush it.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Action becomes free when you stop claiming its results.
snippet: Surrender all actions to me with the disciplined mind. Free from desire, possessiveness, and sorrow, fight.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Trusting the teaching frees action from its binding force.
snippet: Those who always follow this teaching of Mine with trust and without fault-finding are freed from the bonds of action.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Rejecting clear guidance leaves the mind stranded.
snippet: But those who, finding fault with this teaching, do not follow it—know them to be deluded in all knowledge and ruined in understanding.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Nature drives behavior; force alone cannot overrule it.
snippet: Even the wise act according to their own nature. All beings follow nature; what can restraint do?

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom begins when attraction and aversion stop steering action.
snippet: Attachment and aversion sit in every sense-object. Do not come under their power, for they are your enemies on the path.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Imperfect right action is safer than perfect imitation.
snippet: Better is one's own duty, though imperfect, than another's duty well performed. Better death in one's own duty; another's duty is dangerous.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Compulsion has a cause, and Arjuna wants to name it.
snippet: Arjuna said: O descendant of the Vrishni clan, what drives a person to commit wrongdoing, even unwillingly, as if forced?

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire is not harmless; it mutates into the force that ruins judgment.
snippet: Krishna said: Desire, born of rajas, turns into anger. It is all-consuming and deeply destructive. Know it as your enemy here.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear seeing is not destroyed; it is simply covered.
snippet: As fire is covered by smoke, a mirror by dust, and a womb by a membrane, so clear seeing is covered by desire.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire does not just tempt; it hides wisdom itself.
snippet: O son of Kunti, wisdom is covered by this desire, the eternal enemy of the wise, as fire is never satisfied.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire conquers by hijacking the mind's own instruments.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Sense-control comes before clear action.
snippet: Therefore, O best of the Bharatas, first control the senses, and then destroy this great sinner, the destroyer of knowledge and wisdom.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire sits above reason, so mastery must begin earlier.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire loses power when the higher mind takes command.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Timeless wisdom survives by being passed on, not newly invented.
snippet: The Lord said: I taught this imperishable yoga to Vivasvan. Vivasvan taught it to Manu, and Manu taught it to Ikshvaku.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Even timeless wisdom disappears when no one keeps receiving it.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The deepest teaching opens only to devotion and trust.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: The unborn can still appear without ceasing to be free.

speaker: Krishna
essence: When dharma collapses, the divine steps into history.
snippet: Whenever there is a decline of dharma and a rise of adharma, I manifest myself.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The divine returns whenever order must be restored.
snippet: For the protection of the righteous, for the destruction of wrongdoers, and for the establishment of dharma, I appear age after age.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Seeing Krishna truly ends the cycle of return.
snippet: Arjuna, my birth and action are divine. One who truly understands this does not take birth again after leaving the body, but comes to me.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Purified minds reach what fear and craving cannot.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The divine response matches the way you come forward.
snippet: As people approach Me, so I respond to them. All people follow My path in every way, O Arjuna.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire for quick success makes people chase fast-acting powers.
snippet: People who want quick success worship radiant beings, because results from action come quickly in the human world.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The source of order stands outside the order it creates.
snippet: The four social orders were created by Me according to qualities and actions. Though I am their maker, know Me as the unchanging non-doer.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Knowing the action is not yours to possess breaks its grip.
snippet: Actions do not stain me, and I do not crave their fruits. One who knows me this way is not bound by actions.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Right action is the path even for those seeking freedom.
snippet: Even seekers of freedom acted after knowing this. Therefore, do the work that your ancestors always did.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: Action cannot be judged quickly; its true pattern is subtle.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom can live inside action, and bondage can hide inside stillness.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: Real understanding burns the binding power of every action.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Nothing purifies like knowledge, and yoga ripens it from within.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom comes when desire and anger no longer govern the mind.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: A steady mind begins with a steady seat.

essence: Steady posture is the first gate to a steady mind.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: A steady mind reaches the peace that restlessness can never touch.
snippet: The disciplined yogi, always uniting the mind in this way, attains the supreme peace of freedom rooted in Me.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: Yoga begins when craving loses its grip and the mind comes home.
snippet: When the controlled mind rests in the true self alone and is free from craving for all pleasures, that person is called united in yoga.

speaker: Krishna
essence: A trained mind becomes steady enough to stop flickering.
snippet: As a lamp in a windless place does not flicker, so the disciplined mind of a yogi does not waver while practicing yoga of the true self.

speaker: Krishna
essence: True joy is deeper than sensation and keeps you from wavering.

speaker: Krishna
essence: True fulfillment leaves nothing more to chase and nothing strong enough to unsettle you.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Yoga is the breaking of suffering’s grip, practiced steadily.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire loses power when the mind stops feeding it.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Stillness comes by degrees when the mind stops feeding itself.
snippet: Little by little, the mind should be quieted by steady discernment. Fix the mind in the true self and think of nothing else.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The mind is mastered by repeated return, not by force.
snippet: Whenever the restless, unsteady mind wanders, bring it back under the control of the true self.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Joy comes when the restless mind finally becomes still.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: What sustains the world is subtler than what the senses first show.
snippet: Know My higher nature, which is different from this lower nature, O mighty-armed one. By that living nature, this world is sustained.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Everything that appears rises from one source and returns there.
snippet: Know that all beings are born from these two natures. I am the source and the dissolution of the entire universe.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Everything is held in one sustaining reality.
snippet: O Dhananjaya, nothing exists higher than Me. All this world is strung on Me like pearls on a thread.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The sacred is already inside ordinary experience.
snippet: O son of Kunti, I am the taste in water, the light in the moon and sun, the syllable Om in all the Vedas, the sound in space, and the val…

speaker: Krishna
essence: The sacred is already inside nature, vitality, and discipline.
snippet: I am the pure fragrance in the earth, the radiance in fire, the life in all beings, and the austerity in ascetics.

speaker: Krishna
essence: All brilliance is borrowed from the same source.
snippet: Know Me, O Partha, as the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intelligence of the intelligent and the brilliance of the brilliant.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Strength becomes clean when desire no longer fights dharma.
snippet: Among the strong, I am strength free from desire and attachment. In beings, I am desire that does not oppose dharma, O best of the Bharatas.

speaker: Krishna
essence: All qualities arise in the divine, yet the divine is not bound by any of them.
snippet: Know that all states of being—sattvic, rajasic, and tamasic—arise from Me. I am not in them; they are in Me.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Three qualities can hide the imperishable reality from plain sight.
snippet: Deluded by these three qualities, the whole world does not know me, who am beyond them and imperishable.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The way beyond the veil is not force, but refuge.
snippet: My divine illusion, made of the three qualities, is hard to cross. Only those who take refuge in Me go beyond it.

speaker: Krishna
essence: A corrupted mind cannot recognise what would free it.
snippet: The deluded, the lowest among people, the wrongdoers, and those whose understanding has been stolen by the veil do not turn to me. They t…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Different needs can still lead to one real turning toward the divine.
snippet: Four kinds of virtuous people worship Me, Arjuna: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of wealth, and the wise one, O best…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Deep knowing and devoted love become a mutual bond.
snippet: Among those four, the wise devotee who is ever united and one-pointed is श्रेष्ठ. For I am exceedingly dear to the wise one, and the wise…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Deep knowing turns devotion into identity.
snippet: All these are noble indeed, but the wise devotee is My very self. Established in yoga, that person is steadfast in Me as the unsurpassed …

speaker: Krishna
essence: Deep seeing ends in surrender to the one reality in all things.
snippet: After many births, the wise one surrenders to Me, knowing that Vasudeva is everything. Such a great soul is very rare.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire clouds judgment and sends the mind toward smaller refuges.
snippet: Driven by desire, people whose understanding has been taken away turn to other radiant beings. They follow the rules of each, shaped by t…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Faith becomes firm where the devotee turns.
snippet: Whatever form a devotee wants to worship with faith, I make that faith firm in that very form.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Faith draws the result, but the deeper order grants it.
snippet: Endowed with that faith, that person seeks that worship, and receives those desires, granted by Me alone.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Finite worship brings finite results; devotion returns you to Krishna.
snippet: But the reward gained by those of limited understanding is finite. Those who worship the radiant beings go to the radiant beings; my devo…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Form can hide the imperishable reality beneath it.
snippet: The unwise think I have taken a visible form from the unseen. They do not know my imperishable, supreme nature.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The hidden divine is missed by confused seeing, not by absence.
snippet: Veiled by yoga-maya, I am not visible to everyone. Deluded people do not recognize me as unborn and imperishable.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Everything is visible to the divine; the divine remains unseen to ordinary minds.
snippet: Arjuna, I know all beings of the past, the present, and the future. No one knows me.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire and aversion turn clear seeing into confusion.
snippet: O descendant of Bharata, all beings fall into delusion at birth, from the confusion born of desire and aversion, O scorcher of foes.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear hearts stop wavering and move toward the divine with resolve.
snippet: But those whose wrongdoing has been cleared away, and whose good deeds have matured, are freed from confusion and worship me with firm re…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Refuge in the divine opens total understanding of reality, inner life, and action.
snippet: Those who, seeking release from old age and death, take refuge in me, come to know Brahman, the whole field of the inner life, and all ac…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Complete understanding remains steady even at the moment of departure.
snippet: Those who know me together with the physical realm, the radiant powers, and the inner sacrifice know me even at the time of death.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Clear seeing starts by asking what each word truly means.
snippet: Arjuna said: O Supreme Person, what is Brahman? What is the self? What is action? What is called the field of changing things, and what i…

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Real understanding begins by asking what remains when life is ending.
snippet: Arjuna said: O Madhusudana, who is the sacrifice here, and how does he dwell in this body? And at the time of death, how are you known by…

speaker: Krishna
essence: What lasts, what you are, and what you do are not the same.
snippet: The imperishable is the supreme reality. One's own nature is called the inner self. The offering that brings beings into existence is cal…

speaker: Krishna
essence: The changing world, the cosmic order, and the inner witness are not separate.
snippet: The perishable world is the lower realm; the cosmic person is the radiant being. I am the inner witness of sacrifice in this body, Arjuna.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The last remembered presence shapes the next state of being.
snippet: Whoever remembers Me at the end and leaves the body attains My state; there is no doubt.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The end follows the state you have trained.
snippet: Whatever state a person remembers at the end, that state is reached, because the mind has been shaped by it.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Action and remembrance can happen together without conflict.
snippet: Therefore, remember Me at all times and fight. With mind and intellect offered to Me, you will surely come to Me.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What the mind has practiced most will claim you at the end.
snippet: One who departs while remembering the divine person with a mind trained by practice and fixed on nothing else reaches that supreme divine…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Hold the ungraspable source in mind, not the passing form.
snippet: Whoever remembers the wise, ancient, ruler, subtler than the subtle, supporter of all, beyond thought, radiant like the sun, and beyond d…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Steady devotion carries awareness beyond the body's final threshold.
snippet: At the time of departure, one who leaves the body with an unmoving mind, devotion, and the power of yoga, placing the life-force between …

speaker: Krishna
essence: The imperishable is reached by giving up desire, not by feeding it.
snippet: I will briefly tell you that imperishable state, which the knowers of the Vedas call imperishable, which the detached ascetics enter, and…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Mastery of the senses and mind prepares one for the final passage.
snippet: Closing all the doors of the senses, holding the mind in the heart, and placing the life-force in the head, one remains fixed in yoga-dis…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Final remembrance can carry consciousness beyond the body.
snippet: Uttering Om, the one-syllable Brahman, and remembering Me, one who departs while leaving the body attains the supreme state.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Constant remembrance makes the divine easy to reach.
snippet: Arjuna, for the one whose mind is wholly on me and who remembers me constantly, I am easy to reach.

speaker: Krishna
essence: True arrival ends the need to come back.
snippet: Great souls who reach me do not return to this painful, temporary cycle of birth.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Even the highest attainments return, but union with Krishna does not.
snippet: All worlds up to Brahma's realm return again, Arjuna. But having reached me, O son of Kunti, there is no rebirth.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Cosmic time reveals how small ordinary urgency is.
snippet: Those who know that Brahma’s day lasts a thousand yugas, and his night lasts a thousand yugas, know day and night truly.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Everything formed returns to the unmanifest in time.
snippet: At the start of Brahma's day, all beings arise from the unmanifest; at the start of Brahma's night, they dissolve back into that same unm…

speaker: Krishna
essence: All formed things return and rise again under nature’s compulsion.
snippet: O Arjuna, this entire group of beings is born again and again, and is dissolved at the coming of night; at the coming of day, it rises ag…

speaker: Krishna
essence: What is deepest in reality cannot be destroyed by any ending.
snippet: Beyond that unmanifest is another, eternal unmanifest. Even when all beings perish, it does not perish.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The highest arrival is beyond return.
snippet: That is called the unmanifest and the imperishable. It is the highest goal. Having reached it, one does not return. It is my supreme abode.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Undivided devotion reaches the one presence that holds all beings.
snippet: O Arjuna, the supreme person is reached through undivided devotion. All beings dwell within that being, and this entire world is pervaded…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Not every departure leads the same way; some paths return you, others do not.
snippet: I will tell you of the time when yogis do not return, and the time when they do, O best of the Bharatas.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The final passage opens for those who know the supreme reality.
snippet: Fire, daylight, the bright fortnight, and the six months of the northward course: those who depart then, knowing the supreme reality, go …

speaker: Krishna
essence: Some routes only delay return; they do not end the cycle.
snippet: In the path ruled by smoke, night, the dark fortnight, and the six months of the southern course, the yogi who departs there returns afte…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Some choices free you from the cycle; others send you back into it.
snippet: The bright and the dark are the two eternal paths of the world. One leads to non-return; the other brings return again.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Understanding the two paths keeps the mind unshaken.
snippet: O Arjuna, the yogi who knows these two paths is never deluded. Therefore, remain steadfast in yoga at all times.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Knowing the way beyond rewards leads to the highest home.
snippet: Knowing the two paths, the yogi goes beyond all the merits declared in the Vedas, sacrifices, austerities, and gifts, and reaches the pri…

speaker: Krishna
essence: The deepest truth frees only a heart that does not resist it.
snippet: The Blessed Lord said: I will now tell you this most secret knowledge, joined with direct realization, to you who are not envious. Knowin…

speaker: Krishna
essence: The highest truth is both profound and simple to live.
snippet: This is the king of all knowledge, the king of all secrets. It is supremely pure, directly knowable, aligned with dharma, easy to practic…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Without trust, the highest teaching cannot carry you beyond repetition.
snippet: O conqueror of foes, people without trust in this dharma do not reach Me and keep returning on the path of repeated death and rebirth.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The supreme reality holds everything without being held by anything.
snippet: All this universe is pervaded by my unmanifest form. All beings dwell in me, but I do not dwell in them.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The supreme reality sustains all beings without being contained by them.
snippet: Behold My divine power: beings are not in Me, and yet I sustain all beings.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What moves everywhere still rests in the divine.
snippet: As the great wind, moving everywhere, always rests in space, so all beings rest in Me.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What seems like ending is only a return before another beginning.
snippet: O son of Kunti, at the end of a cosmic cycle all beings enter my nature; at its beginning, I send them forth again.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Life unfolds through a power larger than individual will.
snippet: Taking hold of my own nature, I repeatedly create this entire multitude of beings, helpless under the sway of nature.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Action can continue without leaving a chain behind.
snippet: Those actions of creation do not bind me, Dhananjaya, because I remain detached and indifferent to them.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Change is not chaotic; it unfolds within a higher order.
snippet: Nature, under my supervision, brings forth all moving and unmoving beings. Because of this, the world keeps changing, O son of Kunti.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Ordinary appearance can hide the highest reality.
snippet: Fools despise Me when I dwell in a human form, not knowing My higher nature as the great Lord of all beings.

speaker: Krishna
essence: A confused mind turns even hope, effort, and learning into waste.
snippet: Those whose hopes, actions, and understanding are all wasted, confused minds take shelter in deluding, demonic, and shadowy nature.

speaker: Krishna
essence: True devotion begins when the mind recognises the imperishable source.
snippet: But, Arjuna, the great-minded ones, relying on the divine nature, worship Me with one-pointed minds, knowing Me as the unborn and imperis…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Steady devotion becomes a life of continual remembrance.
snippet: Ever united with Me, steadfast in vows, they worship Me by chanting, striving, and bowing with devotion.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The same reality can be approached as one, many, or all-encompassing.
snippet: Others worship Me through the sacrifice of knowledge, seeing Me as one, as separate, or as the all-facing form.

speaker: Krishna
essence: All sacred action is already filled with the divine presence.
snippet: I am the sacrifice, I am the ritual, I am the offering, I am the herb, I am the mantra, I am the ghee, I am the fire, and I am the act of…

speaker: Krishna
essence: All sources of life and meaning are gathered in Krishna.
snippet: I am the father of this world, the mother, the supporter, and the grandfather. I am the object of knowledge, the purifier, the sacred syl…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Everything rests in one imperishable source.
snippet: I am the goal, the supporter, the lord, the witness, the refuge, the friend, the source, the dissolution, the resting place, the treasure…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Opposites do not stand outside the divine; they flow from it.
snippet: I heat the world as the sun, I hold back the rain and send it forth. I am immortality and death, being and non-being, Arjuna.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Rewarded devotion still keeps you bound to return.
snippet: Those who know the three Vedas, drink soma, and are purified by sacrifice pray for heaven. Having earned merit, they enjoy the divine ple…

speaker: Krishna
essence: What is won by wanting is lost by time.
snippet: After enjoying the vast heaven-world, they return to the mortal world when their merit is exhausted. Thus, those who follow the ritual pa…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Unwavering devotion is met by unwavering support.
snippet: Those who think of me alone and worship me with unwavering focus—I carry their welfare and security.

speaker: Krishna
essence: All sincere worship reaches the one reality, even when the form is confused.
snippet: Even those who, filled with trust, worship other radiant beings, worship Me alone, but in the wrong way, O son of Kunti.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Every offering matters only when you know who receives it.
snippet: For I am the enjoyer and lord of all sacrifices. But those people do not know me in truth, and so they fall away.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Devotion does not stay abstract; it carries you to its chosen end.
snippet: Those devoted to the radiant beings reach the radiant beings. Those devoted to the ancestors reach the ancestors. Those devoted to spirit…

speaker: Krishna
essence: A small offering becomes complete when devotion fills it.
snippet: Whoever offers me a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or water with devotion, I accept that devoted offering from the pure-hearted.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Every action becomes complete when it is offered.
snippet: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer, whatever you give, and whatever austerity you practice, offer it to me.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom begins when even the result is no longer yours to carry.
snippet: In this way, you will be freed from the bonds of action and its results. Given over to renunciation-yoga, released, you will come to me.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Equality is universal; intimacy is born through devotion.
snippet: I am equal toward all beings. No one is hated by me, and no one is especially dear. But those who worship me with devotion dwell in me, a…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Undivided devotion can outweigh even a deeply flawed life.
snippet: Even if a person of very bad conduct worships Me with undivided devotion, that person must be regarded as righteous, for the resolve is r…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Devotion quickly changes a person and protects them from ruin.
snippet: One who is devoted to Me quickly becomes righteous and attains lasting peace. O son of Kunti, declare it boldly: My devotee never perishes.

speaker: Krishna
essence: No birth or status can block one who takes refuge.
snippet: O Partha, even those born in difficult circumstances, and women, merchants, and workers, can reach the highest state by taking refuge in me.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What does not last cannot finally satisfy; turn your life toward the divine.
snippet: If even pure Brahmins and royal sages devoted to the divine reach the highest state, what to speak of you? So, having come into this impe…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Total orientation toward the divine becomes the way to reach the divine.
snippet: Fix your mind on me. Be devoted to me. Worship me. Bow to me. United with me in this way, and fully oriented toward me, you will come to me.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Deeper truth arrives as care for the one who is ready.
snippet: The Blessed Lord said: Listen again, O mighty-armed one, to my supreme word, which I will speak for your welfare because you are deeply d…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Even the highest knowers stand inside the source they seek.
snippet: The hosts of devas do not know my origin, nor do the great sages, for I am the beginning of the devas and the great sages in every way.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear recognition of the divine ends confusion and frees action.
snippet: One who knows Me as unborn, beginningless, and the great lord of all worlds is undeluded among mortals and is freed from every wrong deed.

speaker: Krishna
essence: All human qualities arise from the same source.
snippet: Understanding, wisdom, freedom from confusion, forgiveness, truth, self-restraint, calmness, pleasure, pain, coming into being, ceasing t…

speaker: Krishna
essence: All qualities are expressions of the same divine source.
snippet: Nonviolence, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, honor, and dishonor—all these different states of beings arise from Me alone.

speaker: Krishna
essence: All lineages and worlds emerge from Krishna’s mind.
snippet: The seven great sages, the four ancient sages, and the fourteen Manus were born from my mind. The people of these worlds descend from them.

speaker: Krishna
essence: True recognition of Krishna's presence makes devotion unshakable.
snippet: One who truly knows this glory and this yoga of mine becomes steadfast in yoga without doubt.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Knowing the source turns understanding into devotion.
snippet: I am the source of all; everything moves from me. Knowing this, the wise worship me with wholehearted devotion.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Shared remembrance keeps devotion alive.
snippet: With minds fixed on Me and lives offered to Me, My devotees speak of Me to one another, and find continual joy in that shared devotion.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Steady devotion itself draws forth the understanding that reaches the divine.
snippet: To those who are always devoted and worship Me with love, I give the discernment that leads them to Me.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Compassion becomes knowledge that burns away inner darkness.
snippet: To show them compassion, I destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the shining lamp of knowledge, dwelling in their own being.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Recognition turns into surrender when the highest reality stands revealed.
snippet: Arjuna said: You are the supreme reality, the highest refuge, the purest one, the eternal divine person, the first being, unborn, and all…

speaker: Arjuna
essence: The deepest recognition is echoed by the wise and by Krishna himself.
snippet: All the sages speak of you this way: the divine sage Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa. You yourself also say this to me.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: True recognition begins where ordinary beings cannot see.
snippet: I accept as true all that you have said, O Keshava. O Bhagavan, neither the devas nor the asuras know your manifested form.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: The supreme one is known fully only by the supreme one.
snippet: O Supreme Person, you alone know yourself by yourself, O source of all beings, lord of all beings, divine of the divine, ruler of the world.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Only the source can fully name its own presence in the world.
snippet: Only you can fully describe your own divine manifestations, by which you pervade and sustain all these worlds.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Devotion needs a form the mind can return to again and again.
snippet: O Yogi, how shall I know You by constant contemplation? In what forms should You be contemplated, O Blessed One?

speaker: Arjuna
essence: True devotion never feels finished hearing the beloved.
snippet: O Janardana, tell me again in detail of Your yogic power and Your manifestations, for I am not satisfied hearing Your nectar-like words.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The divine can be named briefly, never exhausted.
snippet: Krishna said: I will tell you briefly of my divine manifestations, chief of them, Arjuna, for there is no end to their विस्तार.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Everything that exists is held within one enduring presence.
snippet: I am the Self, O Gudakesha, seated in the heart of all beings. I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Every radiance is a sign of one presence.
snippet: Among the sons of Aditi, I am Vishnu; among radiant things, I am the sun with rays. I am the brilliance in the Maruts, and among stars, I…

speaker: Krishna
essence: The divine is not elsewhere; it is the awareness within everything.
snippet: Among the Vedas, I am the Sama Veda; among the radiant beings, I am Indra. Among the senses, I am the mind; among living beings, I am con…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Greatness in the world is a visible sign of Krishna's presence.
snippet: Among the Rudras, I am Shankara; among the Yakshas and Rakshasas, I am Kubera. Among the Vasus, I am fire, and among mountain peaks, I am…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Greatness in the world is a trace of Krishna everywhere.
snippet: Among priests, know Me as Brihaspati; among commanders, I am Skanda; among waters, I am the ocean.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The divine appears through the noblest forms of speech, ritual, and nature.
snippet: Among great sages I am Bhrigu; among words I am the one imperishable syllable. Among sacrifices I am the sacrifice of silent repetition; …

speaker: Krishna
essence: The finest forms in creation are windows into Krishna.
snippet: Among all trees I am the sacred fig tree. Among divine sages I am Narada; among celestial musicians, Chitraratha; among perfected sages, …

speaker: Krishna
essence: Greatness in the world is a visible trace of Krishna.
snippet: Among horses, know me as Ucchaihshravas, born from nectar. Among royal elephants, I am Airavata, and among humans, I am the king.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Power, fertility, and danger all reveal the same source.
snippet: Among weapons, I am the thunderbolt; among cows, I am the wish-fulfilling cow. I am the cause of procreation, and among serpents, I am Va…

speaker: Krishna
essence: The divine is visible in every order of existence.
snippet: Among serpents I am Ananta; among water beings I am Varuna. Among ancestors I am Aryaman; among those who govern, I am Yama.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Power, devotion, and majesty are all windows into one presence.
snippet: Among the daityas I am Prahlada; among calculators I am Time. Among beasts I am the lion, and among birds I am Garuda.

speaker: Krishna
essence: One reality appears as every noble force.
snippet: Among purifiers, I am the wind. Among wielders of weapons, I am Rama. Among aquatic beings, I am the crocodile. Among flowing rivers, I a…

speaker: Krishna
essence: All creation and all truth-seeking speech point back to Krishna.
snippet: I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all creation, Arjuna. Among all forms of knowledge, I am the knowledge of the true self; a…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Even letters and time carry Krishna's presence.
snippet: Among letters I am the letter A. Among compounds I am the dual compound. I am endless time itself, and I am the all-facing sustainer.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Even death and virtue are expressions of the same divine source.
snippet: I am death that takes everything, and the source of all future beings. Among women, I am fame, fortune, speech, memory, intelligence, ste…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Sacred sound, time, and spring all carry the same presence.
snippet: Among hymns I am the Brihat-Sama; among meters I am Gayatri. Among months I am Margashirsha; among seasons I am spring.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What shines through victory and resolve is not separate from the divine.
snippet: Among deceivers I am gambling, among the vigorous I am splendor. I am victory, I am resolve, and I am the goodness of the good.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Greatness in many forms is one presence showing itself.
snippet: Among the Vrishnis I am Vasudeva; among the Pandavas I am Dhananjaya. Among sages I am Vyasa, and among poets I am Ushana, the poet.

speaker: Krishna
essence: True power is measured by restraint, silence, and clear understanding.
snippet: Among enforcers, I am punishment; among those seeking victory, I am policy. Among secrets, I am silence; among the wise, I am knowledge.

speaker: Krishna
essence: All existence grows from one hidden source.
snippet: Arjuna, I am the seed of all beings. Without me, nothing moving or unmoving could exist.

speaker: Krishna
essence: No list can contain the divine's endless expressions.
snippet: O scorcher of foes, my divine manifestations have no end. What I have described to you is only a brief summary of my vast manifestations.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Every genuine greatness is only a spark of my radiance.
snippet: Whatever being or thing is endowed with splendour, beauty, or power—know that to have arisen from a spark of my radiance.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The whole universe rests in a single fragment of the divine.
snippet: Or, Arjuna, what is the need for all this detailed knowledge? I sustain this entire universe with a single fragment of myself.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Clarity arrives when the hidden teaching is finally heard as personal grace.
snippet: Arjuna said: By the gracious words you spoke for my benefit alone, my delusion has been dispelled.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: All change is clear only beside what does not change.
snippet: O lotus-eyed one, I have heard from You in detail about the arising and passing away of all beings, and also about Your imperishable maje…

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Belief becomes complete only when it wants to see.
snippet: O Supreme Person, everything you have said about yourself is true. Yet I wish to see your divine form.

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Real vision begins when control gives way to asking.
snippet: If you think I can see that form, Lord, then show me your imperishable self, Master of yoga.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The divine cannot be contained in one shape.
snippet: The Blessed Lord said: See, Partha, my forms in hundreds and thousands, diverse, divine, and of many colors and shapes.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The familiar universe is only a small part of what Krishna reveals.
snippet: See the twelve Adityas, the eight Vasus, the eleven Rudras, the two Ashvins, and the forty-nine Maruts. See many wonders never seen befor…

speaker: Krishna

essence: What is eternal gets pulled around by what changes.
snippet: The embodied being in this world is my eternal part, yet it draws the mind and the five senses, which rest in nature.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The body changes, but the carried pattern moves on.
snippet: Just as the wind carries fragrance from its source, the embodied being carries the mind and senses into a new body.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Experience passes through mind and senses; it does not define the true self.
snippet: Taking hold of the mind, the inner being experiences objects through the ears, eyes, skin, tongue, and nose.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What changes is seen by the wise; the true self is not what moves.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Seeing the supreme reality depends on inner purification, not effort alone.
snippet: The striving yogis see this supreme reality abiding in themselves. The unrefined and thoughtless, even while striving, do not see it.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Every brightness in the world points back to one source.
snippet: The radiance in the sun, the moon, and the fire is Mine alone.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Life is nourished from within by the same presence that holds it together.
snippet: Entering the earth, I sustain all beings with my power. Becoming the moon of life-giving essence, I nourish all plants.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The divine is already working inside your body as life itself.
snippet: I become the digestive fire within living beings, joined with the outgoing and incoming breaths, and digest the four kinds of food.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The deepest knowing comes from the one already dwelling within every heart.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What changes is not the whole of you.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Even the imperishable witness is not the final reality.
snippet: The Supreme Self is beyond both the perishable and the imperishable. The imperishable ruler enters the three worlds and sustains them all.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The highest reality lies beyond both change and permanence.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Clear seeing of the Supreme Self becomes total devotion.

speaker: Krishna
essence: True strength looks like forgiveness, steadiness, and no need to dominate.
snippet: Splendor, forgiveness, steadiness, purity, absence of hostility, and lack of pride belong to the one born with divine qualities, O Bharata.

speaker: Krishna
essence: False pride and cruelty reveal a nature already turned away from wisdom.
snippet: O son of Pritha, hypocrisy, arrogance, self-importance, anger, harshness, and ignorance are the marks of one born with demonic qualities.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom grows from one kind of character; bondage grows from another.
snippet: The divine nature leads to freedom; the demonic nature leads to bondage. Do not grieve, Pandava. You are born with the divine nature.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: False vision turns strength into destruction.
snippet: Holding this view, deluded people with ruined discernment and fierce actions arise only to destroy the world.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Endless wanting turns the mind into a home for delusion.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire and anger turn ambition into injustice.

speaker: Unknown
essence: Possession feeds appetite when the mind mistakes gain for completion.
snippet: This much I have gained today; I will get this desire fulfilled. This wealth is mine now, and more will be mine again.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Ego turns achievement into delusion and mistakes possession for mastery.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Pride turns abundance into delusion and makes more wanting feel justified.
snippet: We are rich, surrounded by many people. Who is equal to us? We will sacrifice, give, and enjoy. Deluded by ignorance, they think this way.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire scatters the mind, and confusion turns that scattering into a fall.
snippet: Deluded by many desires, trapped in the net of confusion, and deeply attached to pleasures, people fall into a lower realm of misery.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Pride can hollow out sacred action until only appearance remains.
snippet: Self-important, stubborn, drunk on wealth and praise, they perform ritual in name only, with pretence and without proper method.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Ego makes you fight the divine presence you carry yourself.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Repeated hatred drives a person into worse and worse forms of existence.
snippet: I cast those hateful, cruel, and vile people again and again into demonic births.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Repeated delusion keeps pulling a person farther from the divine.
snippet: Those deluded beings, born again and again into shadowed births, never reach me and sink into a lower state.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Three inner forces open the way to ruin; dropping them protects the self.
snippet: Desire, anger, and greed are the three gates of ruin. Abandon these three.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Freedom from the three dark impulses opens the way to your highest good.
snippet: Freed from these three gates of darkness, a person acts for their own good and reaches the highest state.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Impulse cannot deliver what it promises.
snippet: Whoever sets aside scripture and acts on desire alone does not attain perfection, happiness, or the highest state.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Impulse cannot judge action; a higher standard must.
snippet: Therefore, scripture is your authority for deciding what should be done and what should not be done. Knowing the duties laid out in scrip…

speaker: Arjuna
essence: Faith alone does not reveal its quality; the motive beneath it does.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Faith takes the shape of the nature beneath it.
snippet: The faith of embodied beings is born of their nature. It is of three kinds: sattvic, rajasic, and tamasic. Listen to this.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Trust is not just held; it becomes the person.
snippet: O Bharata, each person's trust matches their inner nature. A person is made of trust; whatever trust he holds, that is what he becomes.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Faith reveals itself in what each person chooses to revere.
snippet: The sattvic worship radiant beings; the rajasic worship yakshas and rakshasas; the tamasic worship spirits and hosts of the dead.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Harsh effort without right guidance can deepen ego instead of cleansing it.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Harshness is not holiness when it destroys the body and ignores what lives within.
snippet: Those who are ignorant and perform terrible austerities without scriptural guidance, wasting away the body’s elements and Me dwelling wit…

speaker: Krishna
essence: Even sacred actions differ; their inner quality decides their worth.
snippet: Food, sacrifice, charity, and austerity are each of three kinds. Listen to these distinctions.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: What excites the senses can quietly produce suffering.
snippet: Bitter, sour, salty, overly hot, pungent, dry, and burning foods are dear to the passionate person. They bring pain, grief, and illness.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What you repeatedly choose to consume reveals the heaviness within.
snippet: Food that is half-cooked, tasteless, foul-smelling, stale, leftover, and impure is dear to people ruled by tamas.

speaker: Krishna
essence: An offering for reward or applause loses its purity.
snippet: But, best of the Bharatas, know that sacrifice to be rajasic when it is performed for reward or for show.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Ritual without trust becomes empty and heavy.
snippet: They call a sacrifice dark when it lacks scriptural rule, food offering, mantra, fee, and trust.

speaker: Krishna
essence: The body itself can become a disciplined offering.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Speech becomes discipline when it tells the truth without creating harm.
snippet: Speech that does not agitate, is truthful, pleasant, and beneficial, together with study and practice, is called austerity of speech.

speaker: Krishna
essence: A trained mind is its own austerity.
snippet: Serenity of mind, gentleness, silence, self-control, and purity of feeling are called mental austerity.

speaker: Krishna
essence: True discipline is clean only when reward is no longer the motive.
snippet: That threefold discipline of body, speech, and mind, performed with supreme trust and without craving results, is called sattvic.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Practice for applause collapses into instability.
snippet: Austerity done for honor, praise, and worship, or with a show of display, is said to be rajasic here. It gives unstable, temporary results.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Pain imposed in ignorance only deepens confusion.
snippet: Austerity done with foolish stubbornness, by torturing oneself or harming others, is called tamasic.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Giving becomes pure when nothing is being bought in return.
snippet: A gift given with the feeling that giving is a duty, offered to a worthy person at the right place and time, is called sattvic.

speaker: Krishna
essence: A gift becomes lesser the moment it starts asking for something back.
snippet: But charity given in return, or for a desired result, and given grudgingly, is said to be rajasic.

speaker: Krishna
essence: A gift can become degrading when respect is missing.
snippet: Charity given at the wrong place and time, to an unworthy recipient, without respect and with contempt, is called tamasic.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: What is real and worthy carries the mark of sat.
snippet: O Partha, the word sat is used for existence itself, for noble being, and for praiseworthy action.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Steady offering makes ordinary action sacred.
snippet: Steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity, and giving is called sat. Action done for that purpose is also called sat.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Trust is what makes action real; without it, effort becomes empty.
snippet: O Partha, sacrifice, charity, austerity, and any deed done without trust are called unreal. Its fruit exists neither here nor after death.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: Renunciation is not one thing; it has distinct forms.
snippet: Arjuna, first hear my settled view on renunciation. Renunciation is said to be threefold.

speaker: Krishna
essence: What purifies you should be done, not dropped.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: The purest action asks for nothing back.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Desire and ego turn action restless, even when it looks productive.
snippet: But action done with desire for pleasure, or with ego, or with great strain, is said to be rajasic.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Blind action begins where clear seeing is absent.
snippet: Action begun in delusion, ignoring its consequences, harm, and one’s own capacity, is called tamasic.

speaker: Krishna

speaker: Krishna
essence: Craving turns action into a chase for reward and emotional swing.
snippet: The doer ruled by craving, chasing results, greedy, violent in nature, impure, and tossed by joy and sorrow is called passionate.

speaker: Krishna
essence: Dullness in the doer makes even action inert and harmful.
snippet: A careless, crude, stubborn, deceitful, lazy, gloomy, and procrastinating doer is called tamasic.
